title: MATS Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded AI Alignment & Security Program in Berkeley or London (Stipend, Housing, Mentorship & $20K Compute Included)
summary: The MATS Research fellowship is a highly competitive, fully funded research and training program designed to prepare the next generation of researchers working on AI alignment, AI security, and frontier AI risk reduction. Hosted in major research hubs such as Berkeley and London, the program provides participants with financial support, mentorship from world-leading AI researchers, and access to substantial compute resources. This fellowship is designed for individuals who want to contribute meaningfully to ensuring advanced artificial intelligence systems are safe, transparent, and beneficial to humanity. Program Overview The MATS Research fellowship is structured as an intensive 10 to 12-week in-person research program that brings together talented fellows and mentors from top AI institutions. Participants work directly with experts from organizations such as: Anthropic OpenAI Google DeepMind Redwood Research ARC and other frontier AI safety groups The program is designed to provide a deeply immersive environment where fellows can focus entirely on research without financial or logistical barriers. Key Program Details Location: Berkeley (USA) or London (UK) Duration: 10–12 weeks Start Date: September 28, 2026 Application Deadline: June 7, 2026 Funding Package Includes: $12,500 stipend $20,000 compute budget Free housing Meals provided Travel costs covered J-1 visa support (if required) Research Tracks Available Fellows can apply to one or multiple research tracks depending on their interests and skills: Empirical Research Theoretical Research Strategy and Forecasting Policy and Governance Systems Security Field-building and Founding Biosecurity Each track is designed to address different dimensions of AI safety, from technical alignment research to governance and societal impact analysis. Mentorship and Research Environment One of the strongest aspects of the MATS Research is its mentorship model. Fellows are paired with experienced researchers from leading AI labs and safety organizations. Mentors provide: Direct research guidance Project direction and feedback Technical and conceptual mentorship Support in publishing and scaling research ideas This mentorship-driven structure allows fellows to rapidly grow their research skills while contributing to real-world AI safety work. Fellowship Benefits The program provides a comprehensive support system so fellows can focus fully on research: Financial and Material Support $12,500 stipend $20,000 compute allocation Free accommodation Free meals Travel reimbursement Academic and Professional Growth Collaboration with top AI researchers Access to cutting-edge AI safety problems Opportunities to publish research papers Exposure to frontier AI labs and institutions Community and Networking Cohort-based learning environment Strong global AI safety community Networking with researchers and organizations in AI governance and technical alignment Application Process The application process is multi-stage and designed to evaluate both technical ability and research potential. Stage 1: General Application Open until June 7, 2026 Takes 1–2 hours Includes background questions and track-specific responses Requires contact details for two references Stage 2: Central Evaluation Applicants who pass Stage 1 proceed to standardized assessments: Empirical Track: research taste and technical implementation tests Policy Governance Track: analytical writing evaluation Other tracks: may proceed directly to Stage 3 Stage 3: Stream Applications Applicants rank specific research streams Submit tailored responses or research proposals May include interviews or work tests Final Offers Decisions finalized in late July / early August 2026 Matching process considers both mentor and applicant preferences Program begins September 28, 2026 Impact and Outcomes The MATS Research has built a strong track record of impact in the AI safety ecosystem: 80% of alumni work in AI alignment, transparency, or security fields 190+ research publications in recent years Over 12,000 citations across research outputs Strong presence in frontier AI organizations and academic institutions Why This Fellowship Matters As AI systems become increasingly powerful, the need for robust safety research grows rapidly. This program is designed to: Develop skilled researchers in AI alignment Support early-career talent in high-impact research Strengthen global AI safety infrastructure Bridge academia, industry, and policy communities Participants often go on to work at leading AI labs or contribute to influential safety research initiatives. Official Application Link Learn more and apply here Conclusion The MATS Research fellowship is a rare opportunity for aspiring researchers to engage directly with frontier AI safety work in a highly supported environment. With full funding, mentorship from top institutions, and access to significant compute resources, it is one of the most impactful programs for those interested in shaping the future of safe artificial intelligence. Click here for more global Fellowship opportunities The post MATS Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded AI Alignment Security Program in Berkeley or London (Stipend, Housing, Mentorship $20K Compute Included) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: European Central Bank Research Analyst Jobs 2026 in Germany: Earn €4,982 Monthly Plus Benefits
summary: The European Central Bank (ECB) is now accepting applications for multiple Research Analyst positions within its Economic Analysis and Policy divisions. This is an exceptional opportunity for talented graduates and early-career professionals who are passionate about economics, finance, public policy, financial stability, international relations, and research. Based in Frankfurt am Main, Germany, successful candidates will join one of the world s most influential financial institutions and contribute directly to the research, analysis, and policy work that supports the European Central Bank s decision-making processes. With a competitive monthly net salary of €4,982 plus benefits, opportunities for career growth, and the possibility of obtaining a permanent contract after three years, this opportunity offers an outstanding career path for aspiring economists and policy professionals. About the European Central Bank The European Central Bank is the central bank responsible for managing the euro, the official currency used by 21 European Union countries. The ECB s primary objective is to maintain price stability across the euro area and protect the purchasing power of the euro. To achieve this, the institution conducts economic research, monitors financial markets, evaluates monetary conditions, and develops policies that support sustainable economic growth and financial stability. The ECB employs professionals from across Europe and offers a highly international, multicultural, and intellectually stimulating work environment. Research Analyst Positions Available The ECB is recruiting Research Analysts across several departments, including: Economics International and European Relations Macroprudential Policy and Financial Stability Monetary Policy Research Successful applicants will work alongside economists, researchers, analysts, and policy experts to support economic and financial analysis that informs ECB policy decisions. What Will Research Analysts Do? Research Analysts play a critical role in supporting evidence-based policymaking at the ECB. Key responsibilities include: Economic and Financial Analysis Monitor economic developments across the euro area. Analyze financial market trends. Support forecasting exercises. Assess economic conditions in both advanced and emerging economies. Data Collection and Management Collect and manage large datasets. Develop data infrastructures. Process structured and unstructured data. Ensure data quality and consistency. Econometric and Quantitative Research Research Analysts will: Develop econometric models. Build analytical tools. Support research projects. Conduct statistical and quantitative analysis. Contribute to evidence-based policy recommendations. Machine Learning and Advanced Analytics Depending on the department, analysts may: Work with machine learning techniques. Utilize deep learning methods. Analyze large-scale datasets. Develop innovative approaches to economic and financial research. Policy Support Analysts will contribute to: Internal policy briefings. Research notes. Technical reports. Official ECB publications. Presentations supporting policy discussions. Benefits of Working at the ECB Successful candidates will receive a highly attractive package. Financial Benefits Monthly net salary of €4,982 Additional employment benefits Stable public institution employment Career Development The ECB actively invests in employee development through: Professional training programs Mentoring opportunities Cross-departmental mobility Exposure to senior policymakers and experts International Work Environment Employees benefit from: Multicultural teams International collaboration Exposure to global economic issues Networking opportunities across Europe Permanent Career Opportunities One of the strongest benefits of this recruitment cycle is that some contracts may become permanent after three years, depending on: Individual performance Organizational needs Budget considerations Part-Time and Flexible Working Options The ECB is also offering flexible arrangements. Some positions are available on a 50% part-time basis, making them particularly suitable for candidates who are: Pursuing a PhD Completing postgraduate studies Engaged in academic research alongside employment Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements. Educational Requirements Candidates must possess: A Bachelor s degree or equivalent in: Economics Finance Econometrics Data Science Mathematics Political Science Or another relevant discipline Experience Requirements Applicants must have: At least two years of practical experience This may include experience gained through: Internships Research assistant positions Work placements Traineeships Professional employment Additionally, candidates must have at least 12 months of experience in: Economic analysis Economic policy Research institutions Central banks International policy organizations Technical Skills Applicants should have advanced experience using: Python R MATLAB Stata They should also possess experience in: Data management Economic modelling Financial modelling Econometric analysis Language Requirements Candidates must demonstrate: Advanced English proficiency (C1 level) Intermediate proficiency (B1 level) in another official EU language Preferred Qualifications While not mandatory, the ECB prefers candidates who have: A Master s degree Knowledge of machine learning techniques Understanding of deep learning applications Experience with advanced quantitative research Recruitment Process The selection process will be conducted remotely and includes: Written assessment Presentation exercise Interview Interview sessions are expected to take place between: 6–10 July 2026 Candidates who are not selected but perform well may be placed on an ECB reserve list and considered for future opportunities. Why You Should Apply Working at the European Central Bank offers a rare opportunity to contribute to policies that affect millions of people across Europe. Research Analysts gain exposure to cutting-edge economic research, collaborate with leading experts, and develop skills that are highly valued in government institutions, international organizations, financial institutions, and academia. For graduates and early-career professionals seeking a prestigious international career in economics, finance, policy, or research, this opportunity represents an outstanding pathway to professional growth. Application Deadline Deadline: 16 June 2026 Interested candidates should submit their applications before the closing date to be considered for these highly competitive positions. Click here for more and to apply Click here for more global job opportunities The post European Central Bank Research Analyst Jobs 2026 in Germany: Earn €4,982 Monthly Plus Benefits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ocean Nexus International Fellowship 2026 for Journalists: Receive $2,000 Funding to Report on Ocean Justice, Equity, and Environmental Change
summary: Applications are now open for the Ocean Nexus International Fellowship 2026, a unique three-month opportunity designed for journalists who are passionate about exploring the connections between ocean governance, environmental justice, social equity, and community-centered sustainability. Created through a partnership between Ocean Nexus and The Uproot Project, this international fellowship seeks to support experienced journalists from outside the United States who are committed to producing impactful stories that highlight how ocean-related policies, environmental challenges, and systemic inequities affect communities worldwide. Selected fellows will gain access to leading researchers, experts, and networks while receiving financial support to pursue in-depth reporting projects focused on ocean justice and equity. About the Ocean Nexus International Fellowship The Ocean Nexus International Fellowship was established to advance transformative conversations around ocean governance, environmental sustainability, and social justice. Ocean Nexus is an initiative dedicated to promoting ocean equity by examining how ocean governance systems impact communities, particularly those that have historically been marginalized or excluded from decision-making processes. Through research, advocacy, and collaboration, Ocean Nexus works to address systemic inequities and hold powerful interests accountable for environmental and social harms. Working alongside Ocean Nexus researchers and experts, fellows will deepen their understanding of the relationship between oceans and social systems. The fellowship encourages journalists to investigate stories that reveal how environmental policies intersect with issues such as: Social inequality Economic injustice Climate vulnerability Community resilience Coastal environmental justice Indigenous and local knowledge systems Ocean sustainability Governance and accountability Participants will also contribute to the broader journalism community by creating resource guides that help other reporters better understand ocean equity issues and improve future reporting on these important topics. Fellowship Objectives The Ocean Nexus International Fellowship is designed around several key goals: 1. Explore the Intersection of Ocean Governance and Social Justice Fellows will engage deeply with Ocean Nexus research to understand how policies and governance structures influence environmental outcomes and social equity. Journalists will examine how decisions regarding oceans, coastlines, fisheries, marine resources, and environmental protection can affect communities differently based on factors such as geography, race, income, and historical exclusion. 2. Amplify Marginalized Voices A central focus of the fellowship is ensuring that communities often left out of environmental decision-making are represented in public discussions. Fellows will investigate how coastal populations, Indigenous communities, vulnerable groups, and historically marginalized populations experience environmental challenges and ocean-related policies. 3. Strengthen Public Understanding Through high-quality journalism, fellows will help bridge the gap between scientific research and public awareness. By translating complex environmental and policy issues into accessible stories, journalists can contribute to informed public conversations and policy discussions surrounding ocean equity and justice. 4. Expand Knowledge on Ocean Equity Reporting Each fellow will produce resources that can support other journalists in understanding and covering ocean equity issues more effectively. These materials will help strengthen the field of environmental journalism and encourage more nuanced reporting on ocean-related challenges. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive a range of professional and financial benefits, including: Financial Support Fellowship stipend of $2,000 Funding provided directly by Ocean Nexus to support fellowship activities and reporting efforts Access to Expertise Participants will gain direct access to: Ocean Nexus researchers Scientists and subject-matter experts Ocean governance specialists Environmental justice practitioners Global networks working on ocean equity Professional Development Fellows will have opportunities to: Deepen expertise in ocean justice reporting Engage with global conversations on environmental equity Build relationships with researchers and policy experts Expand professional journalism networks Community Support Participants will benefit from engagement with The Uproot Project community, which supports journalists covering environmental and climate-related topics. Fellowship Duration The fellowship will run for a period of three months: June 2026 – August 2026 During this period, fellows will work on reporting projects while collaborating with Ocean Nexus researchers and experts. Who Can Apply? To be eligible for the Ocean Nexus International Fellowship 2026, applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Geographic and Citizenship Requirements Applicants must: Be based outside the United States Not be a U.S. citizen Membership Requirements Applicants must be members of: The Uproot Project as of May 15, 2026 OR One of the following collaborating journalism organizations: Arab and Middle Eastern Journalists Association Asian American Journalists Association Association of LGBTQ+ Journalists Indigenous Journalists Association Military Veterans in Journalism National Association of Black Journalists National Association of Hispanic Journalists South Asian Journalists Association Trans Journalists Association Professional Experience Requirements Applicants must have: Between 5 and 15 years of experience covering climate or environmental topics Freelance Journalist Eligibility Freelancers are encouraged to apply. However, freelance applicants must demonstrate a commitment from a news organization willing to publish the work produced through the fellowship. Additional Expectations Applicants should demonstrate: Strong interest in ocean justice and environmental equity Commitment to community-centered storytelling Passion for advancing sustainability through journalism Interest in science-based and policy-focused reporting Required Application Materials Applicants must submit the following documents and materials: 1. Letter of Support A letter from: An editor OR A news organization that regularly works with the applicant The letter must be submitted in PDF format. 2. Resume or Curriculum Vitae Applicants must provide: A current resume or CV Submitted in PDF format 3. Writing Samples Applicants may submit: Up to three writing samples demonstrating reporting experience 4. Statement of Interest Maximum: 500 words The statement should explain: Commitment to ocean equity and justice Alignment with fellowship goals Relevant reporting interests and experiences 5. Reporting Project Proposal Applicants must describe: The story they hope to pursue Preferred reporting format (digital, audio, video, multimedia, etc.) Why the story matters Expected impact of the reporting project 6. Additional Responses Applicants will also be asked about: Fellowship learning objectives Personal connection to the issue or affected community Project timeline Publishing plans Previous environmental reporting experience Important Application Note Applicants should be aware that the fellowship organizers have explicitly stated that Artificial Intelligence (AI) should not be used to complete the fellowship application. According to the program guidelines, responses suspected of being generated by AI may not be considered during the review process. Applicants should ensure that all application materials reflect their own original work, experiences, and perspectives. Application Deadline DEADLINE: June 14 by 11:59pm GMT Click here for more and to apply Applicants are encouraged to submit their materials well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical challenges. Why Journalists Should Apply The Ocean Nexus International Fellowship offers a rare opportunity for journalists to investigate some of the most pressing environmental and social challenges facing communities worldwide. Beyond the financial support, fellows gain access to world-class expertise, valuable professional networks, and an opportunity to contribute meaningfully to global conversations about ocean sustainability, environmental justice, and equitable governance. For journalists seeking to produce impactful stories that connect environmental issues with human experiences, this fellowship provides both the resources and support needed to deepen reporting and drive meaningful public engagement. Interested applicants should prepare their materials and submit their applications before the June 14, 2026 deadline. For more global fellowship opportunities click here The post Ocean Nexus International Fellowship 2026 for Journalists: Receive $2,000 Funding to Report on Ocean Justice, Equity, and Environmental Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Azadi Summer Leadership Program 2026 in India: A Fully Immersive Humanitarian Field Experience in New Delhi and Jaipur for Emerging Global Leaders
summary: The Azadi Summer Leadership Program 2026 is a highly immersive four-week humanitarian field experience organized in India by The Azadi Project . The program is designed for emerging global leaders who are passionate about social impact, community development, gender equity, education, and refugee support work. This intensive leadership experience places selected participants directly into grassroots community settings in New Delhi and Jaipur, where they work alongside experienced practitioners to design, implement, and evaluate real-world social impact programs. The focus is on supporting displaced women, refugee families, and marginalized children through structured educational, psychosocial, and livelihood initiatives. Only three participants are selected globally, making this a highly competitive and deeply personalized leadership opportunity. Program Overview The Azadi Summer Leadership Program is structured as a hands-on field immersion where participants are not observers but active contributors to ongoing community development initiatives. Selected humanitarian leaders will be based in two key locations: 1. New Delhi Field Placement In New Delhi, participants will work in a community center that serves displaced women, refugee families, and children. They are expected to either: Design and implement their own community project, OR Join and strengthen existing programs already running at the center Existing Community Programs in New Delhi Participants may engage in the following initiatives: Mental Health Counseling Services Individual therapy sessions (only certified practitioners can facilitate) Psychosocial Support Groups Group sessions focused on emotional resilience and trauma recovery Education Support for Children After-school academic tutoring for refugee and migrant children (Grades 1–6) Secondary School Academic Support Personalized or small-group learning sessions for adolescent girls Digital Literacy Programs Introduction to computers, tablets, and digital platforms such as YouTube Digital Storytelling Initiatives Empowering refugee women and children to share their stories through media Sexual and Reproductive Health Awareness Educational workshops and awareness sessions Livelihood Skills Training Crochet and stitching programs supporting refugee and migrant women entrepreneurs Participants are actively guided through ideation, project design, implementation, and evaluation in collaboration with the local program team. 2. Jaipur, Rajasthan Field Placement In Jaipur, participants work within gender-sensitive citizenship education programs implemented in Madarsa schools. Key activities include: Supporting classroom-based engagement activities Assisting program facilitators in curriculum delivery Engaging directly with children and educators Contributing to the development of inclusive citizenship education models This placement emphasizes intercultural learning, gender inclusion, and education reform in community school systems. Training and Professional Development Throughout the program, participants receive structured expert-led training in: Development communication strategies Social entrepreneurship and innovation Civil society leadership and governance Community-based participatory program design Impact evaluation and reporting These sessions are designed to strengthen participants’ ability to lead sustainable development initiatives in their home countries and globally. Program Duration Duration: 4 Weeks Start Date: July 2026 Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Must be 18 years of age or older Open to undergraduate and graduate students Young professionals in corporate, development, or humanitarian sectors Strong proficiency in English (Hindi is an advantage but not mandatory) Must possess a valid Indian visa or be an Overseas Citizen of India (OCI holder) Open to global applicants with a strong interest in humanitarian and social impact work Program Costs and Funding Structure Training: Fully covered (pro bono expert training included) Accommodation, Food, and Domestic Travel: Organized and provided by the program Estimated Cost for logistics: Approximately USD 3,500 per participant for the full duration International Travel: Must be arranged and funded by participants Application Process Applicants are required to submit their applications online through Google Forms. Required Documents: Motivation letter Completed application form (including volunteering experience, expectations, and availability) Updated CV or Resume APPLY HERE Key Dates Application Deadline: May 30, 2026 Shortlisted Candidates Notified: June 5, 2026 Final Selection Announcement: June 10, 2026 Program Commencement: July 2026 Conclusion The Azadi Summer Leadership Program 2026 offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with vulnerable communities in India while developing practical leadership, project management, and humanitarian skills. With only three participants selected globally, it is one of the most competitive and immersive field leadership experiences available for aspiring changemakers. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . title: PhD Scholarships at the University of Verona (XLII Cycle, Academic Year 2026/2027): Full Admission Call with 104 Funded Positions
summary: The University of Verona Doctoral Programme Admission Call for the XLII Cycle (Academic Year 2026/2027) is now officially open, offering a significant opportunity for advanced academic training and research in Italy. This comprehensive announcement covers admission into PhD programmes and the awarding of 104 PhD scholarships , making it one of the most competitive and well-funded doctoral calls in Europe. Overview of the PhD Admission Call The University of Verona hereby announces a public selection process for admission to its PhD programmes for the XLII cycle. Successful applicants will gain access to advanced research training across multiple disciplines, with full financial support for selected candidates. Key highlights include: Admission into multiple PhD programmes at the University Allocation of 104 fully funded PhD scholarships Increased financial support for doctoral researchers Reduced administrative and tuition-related costs Structured online application process Application Period and Deadline Applicants must submit their applications within the official timeframe: Opening date: 11 May 2026 Deadline: 9 June 2026 at 12:00 noon (Italian time, GMT+1) Late submissions will not be accepted under any circumstances. Application Process (Step-by-Step Guide) Applicants must complete their submission through the official University of Verona application portal: University of Verona PhD Application Portal Steps to Apply: Visit the official application webpage Register as a new user or log in with existing credentials Select the desired PhD programme under “Registrar’s Office – Admission test – Admission test” Complete the online application form carefully Upload all required academic qualifications in PDF format Ensure each file is clearly named and identifiable Pay the mandatory application fee using PagoPA Submit the completed application before the deadline Application Fees and Costs To participate in the selection process, applicants must pay a non-refundable application fee of €10.00 per application . Additional financial details include: Fee applies per application (maximum of 3 applications allowed) Must be paid before the deadline to avoid exclusion Pre-registration costs: €192.00 regional tax for the right to study €16.00 virtual stamp duty Total: €208.00 (single installment) PhD Scholarships and Financial Support The University of Verona has strengthened its financial support system for doctoral students in this cycle. Scholarship Benefits: Annual PhD scholarship: €17,602.57 gross Paid monthly over the entire duration of the PhD programme Includes INPS social security contributions as required by law Equivalent to an increase of approximately €100 net per month compared to previous cycles Additional Financial Benefits: Full exemption from the annual flat-rate registration fee of €250 Reduced cost of access to doctoral studies Long-term financial stability for researchers Important Notes for Applicants Applications must be completed entirely online All documents must be uploaded in PDF format Applicants may submit up to three applications (each requiring a separate fee) Only complete applications submitted before the deadline will be considered Additional programme-specific documentation is available on individual PhD pages Research and Academic Opportunity Scope The University of Verona PhD programmes cover a wide range of academic disciplines, offering candidates the opportunity to engage in high-level research in a structured European academic environment. Applicants are encouraged to consult individual PhD programme pages for: Research topics Supervisor availability Programme-specific requirements Official Publication Publication date: 12 May 2026 Issuing office: PhD Programmes Office, University of Verona APPLY HERE Institutional Information This opportunity is offered by Università degli Studi di Verona , a leading Italian research university known for its strong academic and scientific output. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here . The post PhD Scholarships at the University of Verona (XLII Cycle, Academic Year 2026/2027): Full Admission Call with 104 Funded Positions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EduSpots Head of Education Job Opportunity in Ghana 2026: Lead Transformative Community-Based Learning and Educational Innovation
summary: Are you an experienced education professional passionate about improving learning outcomes, empowering communities, and driving educational innovation? EduSpots is seeking a highly motivated, creative, and tech-savvy Head of Education to join its leadership team and oversee the development and expansion of its Education Hub in Ghana. This exciting full-time leadership position offers a unique opportunity to shape the future of community-led education, support thousands of learners, and contribute to a growing movement that is transforming educational access and outcomes across Ghana. About EduSpots EduSpots is a Ghana-focused educational organization founded on the belief that communities themselves possess the knowledge, creativity, and leadership necessary to design and sustain transformative learning opportunities. Established in 2015 with the creation of the first community learning hub in Abofour, Ashanti Region, EduSpots has evolved into a powerful grassroots movement focused on community ownership, local leadership, and collaborative learning. Today, the organization has: Established 50 community-owned learning hubs known as Spots Engaged over 700 local leaders called Catalysts Supported thousands of learners known as Sparks Created innovative digital learning platforms, including the EduSpots App and WhatsApp for Learning Developed programs focused on: Early years education Digital skills development Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) Climate action Gender equity Community leadership EduSpots is entering an exciting phase of growth and plans to: Expand from 50 to 100 Spots by 2026 Reach an additional 10,000 learners Scale educational technology innovations through the Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship Launch EduSpots Unlocked to share its educational model with wider development and non-governmental organization communities This growth creates an exceptional opportunity for an educational leader to play a critical role in shaping the organization s future impact. Head of Education Position Overview Job Location Remote (Home-based in Ghana) Preferred location: Accra Some travel required Reporting Line Reports directly to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Employment Type Full-time Salary Gross monthly salary between GHS 8,000 and GHS 12,000 Placement based on qualifications and experience Why This Role Matters The Head of Education position sits at the heart of EduSpots mission. The successful candidate will lead educational strategy, curriculum development, learning innovation, staff management, monitoring and evaluation, and partnership development. This role is ideal for someone who: Lives and breathes education Understands Ghana s educational landscape Has extensive classroom and community education experience Enjoys working with digital learning technologies Can inspire teams and volunteers Believes in community-led educational transformation The successful candidate will help ensure that educational programs remain engaging, impactful, inclusive, and scalable. Key Responsibilities The Head of Education will oversee multiple aspects of EduSpots educational programming and strategic growth. 1. Lead the Education Hub The successful candidate will: Manage the day-to-day operations of the Education Hub Supervise five programme coordinators Line-manage an EcoSTEM specialist Support more than 400 volunteer Catalysts Ensure effective delivery of educational programmes across communities 2. Drive Curriculum Development Responsibilities include: Overseeing curriculum design and improvement Providing educational leadership across learner clubs Ensuring quality educational resources are developed Embedding innovation into learning content Supporting continuous improvement of educational materials 3. Strengthen Educational Quality The Head of Education will ensure the integration of EduSpots ten pedagogical principles: Inclusion Collaboration Creativity Playful learning Digital learning Community connection Learning for transformative action Youth leadership Subject mastery Keeping Spots Safe 4. Support Educational Technology Innovation The role involves supporting and enhancing: EduSpots App development WhatsApp for Learning Online learning courses EduSpots Unlocked initiatives Digital educational content creation 5. Leadership Development The successful candidate will: Contribute to leadership training programmes Support Catalyst development initiatives Help create learning materials for leadership pathways Strengthen youth leadership opportunities 6. Safeguarding and Inclusion Key duties include: Ensuring child protection standards are maintained Embedding safeguarding across all programmes Promoting inclusion in educational planning Overseeing Keeping Spots Safe educational initiatives 7. Educational Resource Development Responsibilities include creating and reviewing: Educational handbooks Learning resources Animations PowerPoint presentations Training materials Programme guides 8. Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning The Head of Education will: Support programme monitoring systems Analyze learning outcomes Contribute to programme reporting Ensure lessons learned improve future delivery Strengthen impact measurement frameworks 9. Staff Development and Leadership The role includes: Managing staff performance Conducting appraisals Leading professional development initiatives Coaching education team members Supporting wider organizational learning 10. Strategic Leadership As a member of the leadership team, the Head of Education will: Provide strategic direction Contribute to organizational growth plans Participate in high-level decision-making Help shape future educational programming 11. Partnership Development The successful candidate will: Build relationships within the education sector Support educational partnerships Engage with foundations and donors Collaborate with corporate partners Strengthen stakeholder engagement Professional Development Opportunities One of the most attractive aspects of this role is the significant professional growth available. The selected candidate will benefit from: Direct coaching from EduSpots CEO Access to learning opportunities through MEST Africa Engagement with the Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship Professional development sessions with Theirworld Learning opportunities through the Dovetail Impact Foundation Exposure to global educational innovation networks The CEO herself brings extensive expertise, including: Global Teacher Prize Finalist recognition UNESCO Youth for Peace Young Leader status Fourteen years of educational experience in Ghana Required Qualifications and Experience Applicants should possess substantial expertise in education and programme management. Essential Requirements Candidates should demonstrate: Significant experience in curriculum and educational resource design Strong programme management skills Experience using digital tools to improve learning outcomes Advanced monitoring, evaluation and learning expertise At least five years of education management experience Experience supervising teams Ability to build systems that improve educational quality Strong facilitation and training skills Experience delivering online education through platforms such as Zoom and WhatsApp Experience working with volunteers Experience working remotely Excellent information technology skills Personal Attributes EduSpots Is Looking For The ideal candidate should possess: Passion for community-led education Commitment to educational equity Strong collaborative mindset Excellent leadership abilities Flexibility and adaptability Strong communication skills Accountability and professionalism Problem-solving ability Self-motivation Capacity to work under pressure Strong alignment with EduSpots values and mission Desirable Qualifications While not mandatory, the following will strengthen an application: Volunteer leadership experience Experience working in multicultural teams Master s degree in a related discipline EduSpots Core Values Successful candidates should demonstrate alignment with EduSpots values: Learning mindset Play Teamwork Caring Daring Community leadership Sustainable impact Passion How to Apply Interested applicants should submit: A tailored cover letter An updated curriculum vitae (CV) Applications should be sent via email to: recruitment@eduspots.org Application Deadline Deadline: 10 June 2026 Candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible before the closing date. Final Thoughts The EduSpots Head of Education role presents a rare opportunity for an experienced education leader to influence educational transformation at scale. By combining community leadership, innovative digital learning, educational excellence, and grassroots engagement, the position offers the chance to create lasting impact for thousands of learners across Ghana. Professionals with strong educational leadership experience, a passion for community-driven development, and a commitment to inclusive learning are strongly encouraged to apply and become part of EduSpots ambitious journey toward reaching more communities and learners in the years ahead. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here . title: France Embassy in Uganda Opens Applications for Fully Funded Master’s Scholarships in France for 2026/2027 Academic Year
summary: The Embassy of France in Uganda has officially launched its call for applications for the 2026/2027 Master’s Degree Scholarship Programme , offering talented Ugandan graduates an opportunity to pursue postgraduate studies at recognized public universities in France. This prestigious scholarship program is aimed at young Ugandan students who already hold a Bachelor’s degree and wish to continue their academic journey through a Master’s degree programme taught either in English or French . The scholarship provides comprehensive financial support, covering tuition fees, international travel, living expenses, health insurance, and several additional benefits designed to ensure a smooth academic and cultural experience in France. About the France Embassy Master’s Scholarship The scholarship initiative is part of the Embassy of France’s commitment to strengthening educational cooperation between France and Uganda while supporting the development of highly skilled professionals who can contribute to Uganda’s growth and development. Selected candidates will have the opportunity to study at public universities in France while benefiting from extensive financial and administrative support throughout their academic stay. The programme seeks to support students pursuing Master’s studies in priority sectors that contribute to sustainable development, innovation, economic growth, and social transformation. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive scholarship package that includes: Financial Support Full tuition fee coverage International return airfare between Uganda and France Monthly stipend to cover living expenses Health insurance coverage Fee Waivers Recipients will benefit from: Visa fee exemption CVEC fee exemption (Student and Campus Life Contribution) Accommodation and Administrative Support The scholarship also offers: Priority access to shared student accommodation Support with administrative procedures throughout the study period Guidance and assistance during settlement in France Cultural and Professional Opportunities Awardees will additionally enjoy: Participation in cultural events and activities Networking opportunities with international students and professionals Exposure to French academic, professional, and cultural environments Eligible Fields of Study Applicants must be seeking admission into a Master’s programme within one of the following areas: Sciences Agriculture Sustainable Development Environment Renewable Energy Tourism and Leisure Gender Studies Economics Engineering Business Administration Sport These disciplines have been identified as priority sectors with strong potential for development impact and international collaboration. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must meet all of the following criteria: Must be a Ugandan citizen. Must possess a Bachelor’s degree in one of the eligible fields before the application deadline. Must secure admission or demonstrate proof of application to a recognized French public university offering a Master’s programme. Important Requirement Candidates are responsible for identifying and applying to their preferred French university before submitting their scholarship application. Proof of university application or admission is mandatory and will form an essential part of the selection process. Required Application Documents Applicants must prepare and submit the following documents: Cover letter Copy of a valid identity document Copy of Bachelor’s degree certificate Proof of application or admission from a recognized French public university Applicants who have not yet received final admission may submit evidence showing that their university application process has already been initiated. A final proof of admission will be required to complete the scholarship selection process. How to Apply Applicants must compile all required documents into one single PDF file and submit it via email. Submission Email SCAC, Service de Coopération et d’Action Culturelle, Embassy of France Email: scac.uganda@gmail.com Applicants will receive an acknowledgment email confirming receipt of their application. Selection Process The selection process will take place in two stages: Stage 1: Application Review The Embassy of France in Uganda will review all submitted applications and assess candidates based on academic qualifications, eligibility, and suitability for the scholarship. Stage 2: Interview Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview at the Embassy of France. The interview stage will allow applicants to further demonstrate their academic goals, motivation, and suitability for postgraduate studies in France. Application Deadline Deadline: June 11, 2026 (Midnight) Applicants are encouraged to begin their university admission process immediately and submit their scholarship applications well before the deadline. Notification of Results All applicants will be informed of the outcome of the selection process by: July 11, 2026 Successful candidates will receive further guidance regarding travel arrangements, visa procedures, enrollment, and scholarship administration. Why You Should Apply This scholarship offers much more than financial assistance. It provides access to: High-quality French higher education International academic exposure Professional networking opportunities Cultural exchange experiences Global career development prospects Full financial support throughout the Master s programme For Ugandan graduates seeking advanced education in some of the world s leading universities, this scholarship presents an exceptional opportunity to gain internationally recognized qualifications while experiencing life in France. For more details on this scholarship, visit here Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY, click here The post France Embassy in Uganda Opens Applications for Fully Funded Master’s Scholarships in France for 2026/2027 Academic Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: F5 Opens Applications for 2026 STEM & AI Education Grants: $50,000 Funding Available for Nonprofits Across Africa, Asia, and Latin America
summary: The F5 Foundation has officially launched applications for its highly anticipated 2026 STEM AI Education Grant Program , offering ten nonprofit organizations the opportunity to receive $50,000 USD in unrestricted funding to expand access to STEM and Artificial Intelligence (AI) education among underserved populations living in poverty. Building on six years of successful global STEM grantmaking, the 2026 edition introduces significant enhancements designed to create deeper impact in regions with the greatest educational and technological needs. The program focuses specifically on empowering nonprofit organizations that are preparing future innovators, changemakers, and technology leaders through STEM and AI education initiatives. With artificial intelligence rapidly transforming industries worldwide, F5 is prioritizing organizations that equip vulnerable communities with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities needed to thrive in an increasingly digital economy. About the 2026 F5 STEM AI Education Grants The F5 Foundation’s STEM AI Education Grant Program aims to support organizations that are creating pathways into science, technology, engineering, mathematics, and artificial intelligence careers for individuals living in poverty. For 2026: Grant Amount: $50,000 USD Number of Awards: 10 nonprofit organizations Funding Type: Unrestricted one-time grant Selection Process: Applications reviewed by a global committee of F5 employees Unlike many restricted grants, the F5 grant provides organizations with flexibility in utilizing funds to maximize their impact and strengthen long-term sustainability. What s New in the 2026 F5 Grant Program? The 2026 funding cycle introduces three major strategic changes designed to increase impact and reach. 1. Geographic Focus on High-Need Regions For the first time, F5 is concentrating its funding on regions where access to STEM and AI education remains limited. Eligible regions include: Africa Northern Africa Sub-Saharan Africa Asia Central Asia Eastern Asia South-Eastern Asia Southern Asia Western Asia (Middle East) Latin America Central America South America Caribbean nations This targeted approach aims to direct resources where they can have the greatest transformative effect. 2. Artificial Intelligence is Now a Required Component One of the most significant updates for 2026 is the introduction of a mandatory AI component. To qualify, organizations must demonstrate how AI is integrated into: Educational programs Training initiatives Learning curricula Capacity-building activities Technology-focused career development programs Examples may include: AI literacy training Machine learning education Responsible AI instruction AI-enabled workforce development Practical AI tools for learning and innovation F5 believes AI literacy is becoming essential for future economic participation and seeks to support organizations preparing underserved communities for AI-driven industries. 3. Stronger Focus on Poverty Alleviation The Foundation is placing increased emphasis on serving individuals living in poverty. Organizations must demonstrate that: More than 50% of beneficiaries live in poverty. Programs directly support underserved populations. STEM and AI education activities reduce barriers to opportunity. Participants gain access to educational and career pathways. According to F5, this strategic focus is intended to create more equitable access to emerging technologies and future employment opportunities. Eligibility Requirements Organizations interested in applying must satisfy all eligibility criteria. Organizational Requirements Applicants must: Be a legally registered charitable organization or operate under a registered fiscal sponsor. Have at least three years of operational experience. Maintain an active organizational website. Operate within eligible regions. Focus on STEM and AI education initiatives. Serve populations living in poverty. Demonstrate measurable impact through quantitative evidence. Programmatic Requirements Organizations should focus on one or more of the following: Expanding STEM access AI education Workforce development Capacity building Career readiness programs Technology training Innovation-focused learning opportunities The organization s mission should clearly align with increasing inclusion and opportunity in STEM and AI fields. Non-Discrimination Policy Organizations with policies that discriminate based on: Race Ethnicity Religion Gender Sexual orientation Gender identity Disability Age National origin Political ideology Veteran status or other protected characteristics are not eligible to receive funding. International Organizations Can Apply Nonprofits outside the United States are encouraged to apply. International applicants will undergo an Enhanced Expenditure Responsibility (EER) process administered through CAF America. Importantly: F5 covers all EER fees. Successful completion of the EER process is required before grant disbursement. What Types of Impact Does F5 Want to See? A key component of the application is demonstrating measurable impact. Organizations must provide quantitative evidence showing how their programs have improved STEM and AI access and outcomes. Examples include: Education Outcomes Graduation rates College enrollment rates STEM degree participation Career Outcomes Employment rates Internship placement rates Career advancement statistics Learning Outcomes Improved academic performance Increased STEM competency scores Enhanced digital literacy metrics Participation Outcomes Program completion rates Growth in participant numbers Increased representation of underserved populations Applications supported by strong data are expected to be significantly more competitive. Application Questions Overview The application covers six major areas: 1. Organizational Profile Applicants will provide information about: Legal name Mission Organizational goals Website Regions served Registration country Staffing levels Annual budget 2. Programmatic Focus and Impact Organizations must explain: Current STEM and AI programs Communities served Poverty-focused interventions Measurable outcomes Success stories and case studies 3. Community Partnerships Applicants should highlight: Local partnerships Collaborations Shared initiatives Community engagement strategies 4. Funding and Sustainability Organizations will need to describe: How grant funds will be used Expected outcomes Sustainability plans Future scaling strategies Long-term impact goals 5. Eligibility Confirmation Applicants must formally confirm compliance with all eligibility requirements. 6. Contact Information Organizations will provide: Primary contact person Contact email Referral source Previous participation history Important Dates Application Opening Date June 1, 2026 Application Deadline June 14, 2026 (11:45 PM PST) Notification of Results July 2026 Organizations are strongly encouraged to prepare their responses in advance since the online application cannot be saved and resumed later. Application Guidelines Before applying, organizations should note: Applications must be completed entirely in English. Applications are submitted electronically. Maximum response length is 4,000 words per question. Total application character limit is 200,000 characters. Impact data should be prepared beforehand. Organizations should carefully review all questions and draft responses offline before beginning the submission process. Apply Here Why This Grant Matters The rapid rise of artificial intelligence is creating new opportunities—and new inequalities—across the globe. Through the 2026 STEM AI Education Grant Program, the F5 Foundation is investing in organizations that are helping underserved communities gain access to the skills, knowledge, and opportunities needed to participate in the future economy. For nonprofits already delivering measurable STEM and AI education outcomes, this unrestricted funding represents an opportunity to expand programming, reach more beneficiaries, strengthen sustainability, and accelerate long-term impact. With ten grants of $50,000 available, organizations across Africa, Asia, and Latin America have a unique opportunity to secure support for innovative initiatives that are transforming lives through education and technology. Application Deadline: June 14, 2026 (11:45 PM PST). For more information on this grant, click here Discover more grant opportunities on OFY, click here The post F5 Opens Applications for 2026 STEM AI Education Grants: $50,000 Funding Available for Nonprofits Across Africa, Asia, and Latin America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FAO Global Youth Contest 2026: Win a Fully Funded Trip to Rome for the World Food Forum
summary: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), through the FAO elearning Academy and the World Food Forum (WFF), has launched the Global Youth Contest: From Learning to Impact 2026 , a prestigious international competition designed to showcase how young people are transforming knowledge into meaningful action. The contest offers an exciting opportunity for young learners and professionals worldwide to share how courses completed through the FAO elearning Academy have contributed to their personal growth, academic development, professional advancement, and community impact. Three outstanding winners will be selected to receive a fully funded trip to Rome, Italy , where they will participate in the World Food Forum 2026 , one of the world s leading platforms for youth engagement in sustainable agrifood systems. Applications are open until 31 July 2026 (23:59 CEST) . About the FAO Global Youth Contest 2026 The Global Youth Contest seeks to celebrate inspiring stories of learning and impact from young people who have benefited from the FAO elearning Academy. The initiative aims to demonstrate how digital learning can empower individuals to acquire valuable knowledge, develop practical skills, and create positive change within their communities and professional environments. Participants are invited to submit a compelling written story explaining: How the FAO elearning Academy has influenced their educational or professional journey. The skills and knowledge gained through FAO courses. How they have applied their learning in real-world situations. The impact their learning has generated within their community, workplace, organization, or field of study. Applicants must support their stories with verified course completion records and certified digital badges earned through the FAO elearning Academy. Why You Should Apply The FAO Global Youth Contest offers much more than recognition. 1. Win a Fully Funded Trip to Rome Three winners will receive: Round-trip international travel Accommodation Participation costs Access to the World Food Forum 2026 The World Food Forum will take place in Rome, Italy, from 12–15 October 2026 . 2. Gain International Visibility Selected winners will be featured through: Video interviews FAO elearning Academy website FAO Education Hub YouTube Channel FAO social media platforms This exposure can significantly strengthen participants professional profiles and networks. 3. Share Your Story Globally The contest provides a platform for young people to inspire others through their experiences and achievements. 4. Join a Global Community of Changemakers Participants become part of a worldwide movement focused on: Sustainable agriculture Food security Climate resilience Rural development Sustainable agrifood systems 5. Showcase Your Impact The contest recognizes individuals who have successfully translated learning into action and tangible outcomes. Objectives of the Contest The FAO Global Youth Contest aims to: Highlight Stories of Impact Showcase how FAO learning opportunities are creating meaningful change globally. Promote Learning-to-Action Approaches Demonstrate practical applications of knowledge acquired through FAO courses. Encourage Peer Learning Enable young people to learn from one another’s experiences and innovations. Amplify Youth Voices Increase global awareness of youth-led solutions in agriculture and food systems. Support Sustainable Development Recognize young leaders contributing to more sustainable, inclusive, and resilient agrifood systems. Who Can Apply? To be eligible, applicants must: Age Requirement Be between 18 and 35 years old . Registration Requirement Be a registered learner of the FAO elearning Academy. Learning Requirement Applicants must demonstrate that FAO elearning Academy courses have contributed to: Knowledge acquisition Skills development Academic growth Professional advancement Community impact Digital Badge Requirement Applicants must: Complete FAO elearning Academy courses. Obtain certified digital badges. Travel Requirement Selected winners must possess: A passport valid until at least April 2027 . Eligible Countries The contest is open globally. Applicants can come from: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Oceania Caribbean nations Small Island Developing States FAO specifically encourages participation from: Underrepresented groups Vulnerable communities Marginalized populations Contest Prize Three winners will receive: Fully Funded Participation at the World Food Forum 2026 The prize includes: International airfare Accommodation Participation costs Attendance at the World Food Forum in Rome Global Recognition Winners will be featured in: Professional video interviews FAO elearning Academy homepage FAO Education Hub Official FAO communication channels Evaluation Criteria Applications will be reviewed based on: 1. Availability for the Award Process Applicants must be able to participate in: Interviews Travel requirements World Food Forum activities 2. Number of Digital Badges Earned A stronger portfolio of completed courses and badges may strengthen an application. 3. Quality of the Impact Story Reviewers will assess: Clarity Authenticity Demonstrated impact Practical application of learning 4. Course Completion Verification Applicants must have verifiable completion records for FAO Academy courses. 5. Inclusion Considerations Additional consideration may be given to applicants from: Vulnerable groups Marginalized communities Underrepresented populations How to Apply Step 1: Create an FAO elearning Academy Account If you are not already registered, create an account on the FAO elearning Academy platform. Step 2: Complete Courses Enroll in courses relevant to your interests and career goals. Step 3: Earn Digital Badges Successfully complete courses and collect certified digital badges. Step 4: Prepare Your Impact Story Write a compelling story (maximum 4,000 characters) describing: Your learning journey Skills acquired Career or academic development Community impact Real-world application of knowledge Step 5: Complete the Application Form Submit: Your personal information FAO Academy account email List of earned badges Impact story Step 6: Submit Before the Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 31 July 2026 (23:59 CEST) Apply Here Important Rules Applicants should note that: Only one application is permitted per participant. Applications must be submitted in English. AI-generated responses are strictly prohibited. False information may lead to disqualification. Applicants must own the rights to submitted content. Winners must participate in promotional activities and interviews. Why This Opportunity Matters The FAO Global Youth Contest 2026 offers a rare opportunity for young people to gain international recognition while demonstrating how education can drive meaningful impact. Whether you are a student, researcher, entrepreneur, agricultural professional, development practitioner, or community leader, this contest provides a global platform to showcase your achievements and connect with fellow changemakers working toward more sustainable agrifood systems. Young people who have actively engaged with the FAO elearning Academy and transformed knowledge into action should strongly consider applying for this prestigious opportunity. Application Deadline 31 July 2026 (23:59 CEST) For more information on this competition visit here Discover more opportunities on OFY on here The post FAO Global Youth Contest 2026: Win a Fully Funded Trip to Rome for the World Food Forum appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Gothenburg Doctoral Position in Peace and Development Research 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Sweden on Host-State Responses to Transnational Repression
summary: The University of Gothenburg, one of Scandinavia s leading research-intensive universities, has announced an exceptional doctoral opportunity for aspiring researchers interested in peace, development, migration, human rights, governance, and security studies. The institution is inviting applications for a fully funded Doctoral Student Position in Peace and Development Research with a specialized focus on Host-State Responses to Transnational Repression (TNR). This prestigious PhD opportunity is hosted by the School of Global Studies at the University of Gothenburg and forms part of an international research project examining transnational repression and the responses of host states across the Nordic-Baltic region. The successful candidate will join a vibrant international research environment and contribute to cutting-edge scholarship on one of the most important emerging issues in global politics, migration, democracy, and human rights. Deadline: 14 August 2026 Interested candidates must submit their complete application no later than 14 August 2026 . About the University of Gothenburg The University of Gothenburg is one of Sweden s largest and most respected higher education institutions. With approximately 58,000 students and over 6,800 employees, the university provides a dynamic environment for learning, innovation, and research. Known for addressing major societal challenges through interdisciplinary knowledge and international collaboration, the university attracts talented researchers and students from around the world. Its commitment to academic excellence, diversity, inclusion, and global engagement makes it an attractive destination for doctoral candidates seeking world-class research training. About Peace and Development Research at the University of Gothenburg Peace and Development Research at the University of Gothenburg is internationally recognized as a leading interdisciplinary research environment. The department includes approximately 50 researchers who employ diverse theoretical and methodological approaches to investigate critical global challenges. Research themes include: Peace and conflict Security studies Human rights Gender and development Migration and diaspora studies Global governance Regional governance Political economy Resistance and social movements International relations Democracy and authoritarianism The doctoral candidate will become part of this thriving academic community and contribute to internationally relevant research. Understanding the Research Focus: Host-State Responses to Transnational Repression The advertised doctoral project focuses on Transnational Repression (TNR), a growing phenomenon attracting significant international attention. Transnational repression refers to actions undertaken by authoritarian governments to intimidate, silence, punish, monitor, or undermine dissidents, activists, journalists, and political opponents living outside their home countries. The doctoral research will be conducted within a larger international project examining: How transnational repression occurs across borders How democratic host states respond to these threats Legal frameworks addressing transnational repression Public policy responses Institutional awareness and preparedness Protection mechanisms for vulnerable diaspora communities Possible Research Areas Applicants are encouraged to develop innovative research proposals connected to host-state responses to transnational repression. Potential areas of investigation include: 1. Awareness Among Government Institutions Research may examine awareness levels among: Law enforcement agencies Immigration authorities Border control institutions Cybersecurity agencies National security organizations 2. Government Outreach to Vulnerable Diasporas Candidates may investigate: State engagement with migrant communities Protection initiatives Trust-building measures Community resilience strategies 3. Collaboration Between Government and Civil Society Research could focus on: Partnerships between public institutions and NGOs Advocacy networks Human rights organizations Community support structures 4. Reporting Mechanisms for TNR Incidents Potential topics include: Incident reporting procedures Victim support systems Accessibility of reporting channels Institutional responsiveness 5. Investigation and Prosecution of Perpetrators Candidates may explore: Criminal justice responses Legal frameworks Judicial processes International cooperation mechanisms 6. Other Relevant Topics The university encourages applicants to propose innovative research ideas related to transnational repression and democratic governance. Comparative Nordic-Baltic Research Framework The doctoral project will adopt a comparative approach focusing on two or three Nordic-Baltic countries. Possible case countries include: Sweden Norway Estonia Applicants are encouraged to justify their case selection and explain how comparative analysis can contribute to advancing academic understanding of transnational repression. Structure of the Doctoral Programme The doctoral programme spans four years of full-time study and leads to the award of a Doctoral Degree (PhD). Programme Components Coursework The first year includes structured doctoral-level coursework designed to strengthen theoretical, methodological, and analytical competencies. Research Activities Three years are primarily devoted to: Data collection Fieldwork Comparative analysis Academic writing Conference participation Publication development Dissertation The programme culminates in the production of an original doctoral dissertation that makes a significant scholarly contribution to Peace and Development Research. Employment and Funding Upon admission, the successful candidate will be employed by the University of Gothenburg as a doctoral student. Benefits include: Fully funded doctoral position Monthly salary according to University of Gothenburg regulations Access to university resources and facilities Participation in international research networks Academic supervision by leading scholars Professional development opportunities Possibility of teaching experience The university applies local doctoral salary agreements and provides employment benefits associated with Swedish higher education institutions. Teaching and Departmental Responsibilities Doctoral students may undertake departmental duties corresponding to up to 20% of full-time employment. These responsibilities may include: Teaching undergraduate courses Assisting in graduate-level instruction Research support activities Administrative responsibilities Such duties extend the total study period accordingly. Required Qualifications Applicants must satisfy both general and specific doctoral admission requirements. General Eligibility Candidates must have: A Master s degree or equivalent second-cycle qualification, or At least 240 higher education credits including 60 second-cycle credits, or Equivalent qualifications obtained internationally Specific Eligibility Applicants should possess at least 60 credits in one of the following areas: International Relations Peace and Conflict Studies Global Development Studies Equivalent academic backgrounds from Sweden or abroad will also be considered. Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Excellent written English Excellent spoken English Proficiency in relevant languages connected to selected case-study countries Strong communication skills are essential for conducting comparative international research. Application Assessment Criteria Applications will undergo a highly competitive evaluation process. Reviewers will assess: Research Proposal Applicants must submit a research proposal of up to 2,000 words that includes: Research problem Purpose and objectives Research questions Theoretical framework Methodology Research design Data sources Timeline Harvard-style references Academic Publications and Thesis The selection committee will evaluate: Master s thesis quality Academic publications Research reports Other scholarly works Curriculum Vitae The CV should demonstrate: Academic achievements Research experience Professional experience Language competencies Relevant technical skills Academic Performance Grades obtained during undergraduate and postgraduate studies will be considered. Interviews Shortlisted candidates will be invited to participate in interviews. Selection Timeline The recruitment process follows the schedule below: Application Deadline: 14 August 2026 Shortlisting Decisions: September 2026 Final Decisions: October 2026 Latest Starting Date: January 2027 Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: Research Proposal (maximum 2,000 words) Master s Thesis Relevant Academic Publications or Research Outputs Curriculum Vitae (Europass format recommended) Academic Diplomas Academic Transcripts Evidence of Additional Qualifications Contact Details of Two References Please note that recommendation letters will not be considered. Living and Researching in Sweden The University of Gothenburg provides support services for international students and researchers relocating to Sweden. Benefits of living in Sweden include: High quality of life Strong research infrastructure Excellent work-life balance International academic community Family-friendly environment Safe and inclusive society The university actively promotes diversity, equality, and inclusion across all aspects of academic life. Why You Should Apply This doctoral opportunity offers a unique chance to contribute to one of the most urgent and rapidly evolving areas of global governance and human rights research. By joining the University of Gothenburg, successful candidates will gain access to a leading international research environment, receive full financial support throughout their doctoral studies, and contribute to scholarship with real-world policy relevance. For aspiring researchers interested in migration, authoritarianism, human rights, democracy, and international relations, this fully funded PhD position represents an outstanding opportunity to launch a successful academic career while producing impactful research on a globally significant issue. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global PhD opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post University of Gothenburg Doctoral Position in Peace and Development Research 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Sweden on Host-State Responses to Transnational Repression appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
